{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Business Partner",
  "description": "Del-York Group is a global community of internationally renowned talent, united in our passion for a prosperous and limitless Africa.Our playground is Africa, but our influence is global. From cultivating our creativity to extracting our precious metals, we transform raw resources and human capital into refined products ready for the world. We maximize our capabilities to make a significant impact, empowering global businesses and driving progress around the globe. We are the bridge the global business world and Africa, fostering resilient growth and a strong, interconnected future.

Del-York Group is a dynamic conglomerate of diverse companies, preparing and developing talents and resources for the future of industry and public-private partnerships in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Business Partner
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Description


	Human Resource Business Partner primary responsibilities will involve partnering with business leaders to drive people strategies, while identifying training needs, designing and implementing impactful learning programs, and embedding development initiatives into performance management and employee growth frameworks.
	This role requires a strong blend of business partnering capability, facilitation and instructional design expertise, and a deep understanding of HR principles and adult learning within a dynamic and creative environment.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement learning and development strategies aligned with the academy&rsquo;s mission, business goals, and workforce capability needs.
	Conduct training needs analysis (TNA) to identify skill gaps and design targeted learning interventions across teams.
	Design, deliver, and evaluate training programs, workshops, and learning experiences (technical, behavioural, and leadership development).
	Manage and continuously improve onboarding and induction programs to ensure a strong learning foundation for new hires.
	Support performance management by integrating learning solutions into goal setting, performance reviews, and individual development plans (IDPs).
	Facilitate career development initiatives, including coaching, mentoring, and succession planning frameworks.
	Drive employee engagement through learning-focused initiatives that enhance productivity, morale, and retention.
	Collaborate with department heads to build team capability and ensure learning solutions address real business challenges.
	Monitor and evaluate training effectiveness using KPIs, feedback, and performance data to ensure ROI on learning interventions.
	Maintain learning records, track training participation, and generate insights using HRIS/LMS platforms.
	Champion a culture of continuous learning, knowledge sharing, and professional growth across the organization.
	Support organizational change and transformation initiatives through targeted learning and upskilling programs.
	Stay updated on industry trends, modern learning methodologies, and digital learning tools to enhance training delivery.
	erform other People Operations related roles as assigned.


Skills and Qualification


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or higher in a relevant field, such as Human Resource Management, Business administration, Organizational behaviour or a related field. (A Master&rsquo;s Degree or professional certification in SHRM or any globally recognized HR body may be an advantage).
	Proven experience with over 6 years of progressive experience as an HR Business Partner, Learning and Development or similar role.
	In-depth knowledge of HR principles, labour laws, and best practices.
	Strong interpersonal, networking and communication skills to effectively engage with employees at all levels.
	Excellent organizational abilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Proficient in using HR information systems and other relevant software.
	Problem-solving mindset and ability to make sound decisions.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality in handling sensitive employee information.
	Excellent organizational and people management.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-16",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-31",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Human Resource Business Partner Job at Del-York Group




        
    

        
        Del-York Group is a global community of internationally renowned talent, united in our passion for a prosperous and limitless Africa.Our playground is Africa, but our influence is global. From cultivating our creativity to extracting our precious metals, we transform raw resources and human capital into refined products ready for the world. We maximize our capabilities to make a significant impact, empowering global businesses and driving progress around the globe. We are the bridge the global business world and Africa, fostering resilient growth and a strong, interconnected future.

Del-York Group is a dynamic conglomerate of diverse companies, preparing and developing talents and resources for the future of industry and public-private partnerships in Africa.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Business Partner
Job Location: Lagos

Description


	Human Resource Business Partner primary responsibilities will involve partnering with business leaders to drive people strategies, while identifying training needs, designing and implementing impactful learning programs, and embedding development initiatives into performance management and employee growth frameworks.
	This role requires a strong blend of business partnering capability, facilitation and instructional design expertise, and a deep understanding of HR principles and adult learning within a dynamic and creative environment.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement learning and development strategies aligned with the academy’s mission, business goals, and workforce capability needs.
	Conduct training needs analysis (TNA) to identify skill gaps and design targeted learning interventions across teams.
	Design, deliver, and evaluate training programs, workshops, and learning experiences (technical, behavioural, and leadership development).
	Manage and continuously improve onboarding and induction programs to ensure a strong learning foundation for new hires.
	Support performance management by integrating learning solutions into goal setting, performance reviews, and individual development plans (IDPs).
	Facilitate career development initiatives, including coaching, mentoring, and succession planning frameworks.
	Drive employee engagement through learning-focused initiatives that enhance productivity, morale, and retention.
	Collaborate with department heads to build team capability and ensure learning solutions address real business challenges.
	Monitor and evaluate training effectiveness using KPIs, feedback, and performance data to ensure ROI on learning interventions.
	Maintain learning records, track training participation, and generate insights using HRIS/LMS platforms.
	Champion a culture of continuous learning, knowledge sharing, and professional growth across the organization.
	Support organizational change and transformation initiatives through targeted learning and upskilling programs.
	Stay updated on industry trends, modern learning methodologies, and digital learning tools to enhance training delivery.
	erform other People Operations related roles as assigned.


Skills and Qualification


	Bachelor’s Degree or higher in a relevant field, such as Human Resource Management, Business administration, Organizational behaviour or a related field. (A Master’s Degree or professional certification in SHRM or any globally recognized HR body may be an advantage).
	Proven experience with over 6 years of progressive experience as an HR Business Partner, Learning and Development or similar role.
	In-depth knowledge of HR principles, labour laws, and best practices.
	Strong interpersonal, networking and communication skills to effectively engage with employees at all levels.
	Excellent organizational abilities with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Proficient in using HR information systems and other relevant software.
	Problem-solving mindset and ability to make sound decisions.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality in handling sensitive employee information.
	Excellent organizational and people management.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Executive",
  "description": "Zamiva Transnational Services is a full-fledged human capital development and management consultancy servicing company incorporated in August, 2021 with its corporate Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to ensuring quality value addition to our clients&rsquo; businesses spanning all sectors of the economy in the areas of HR consulting, outsourcing, talent acquisition, executive search, employee/reference background verification, payroll management, learning &amp; development, renting, catering and janitorial services, etc., leveraging research, technology, a highly skilled and passionate human capital base, and our core values of Collaboration, Leadership, Integrity, Continuous Improvement, High Performance and Expertise.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Assists the the Manager in leadingand implementing the procurement of materials, components, equipment, and services at favorable pricing.
	Ensures suppliers are consistent with scheduled deliveries and maintain specifications for applicable materials/equipment.
	Supports in supplier selection and pre-qualification process.&nbsp;
	Investigates and recommends sources of supply and purchases through contractual arrangements to affect cost savings.
	Obtains sources, quotes, generates, and processes requisitions, and maintains related records, logs, files, artwork as pertained to the purchasing department.
	Check all purchasing invoices for accuracy in billing.
	Presents vendor cost comparison reports to upper management as requested. &bull; Monitor and forecast upcoming levels of demand and track inventory levels.
	Other assigned tasks as necessary assigned by Management.
	Follows all safety regulations and complying with SOP&#39;s, EPA, OSHA, FDA, and cGMP&#39;s while performing duties. Maintain cleanliness and appearance of work area.
	Well versed in analysis of spend data and cost models for continuous improvement.
	Maintain relationships with various functional groups across the organization including Manufacturing, Quality, Distribution, and Administration.
	Other duties as assigned. Required


Required Education and Experience


	BSC / HND in any relevant field. However, a practical pharmaceutical manufacturing procurement experience is an advantage
	2 - 3 years of Procurement experience
	Extensive knowledge of cGMP, FDA, and other pharmaceutical requirementsis an advantage
	Experience of supply chain systems in developing contexts and logistics.
	Successful experience in audit/assessments and the implementation of supply chain management improvementsis an advantage.
	Ability to identify trends withinindustry.
	Ability to establish supplier specifications, measure conformance, and identify process improvement opportunities.
	Knowledge of risk management, with the ability to identify and address anticipated issues. &bull; Ability to work independently and troubleshoot challenges.
	Must possess strong verbal, written, and oral communication skills.
	Must be able to work independently and with a team.
	Must possess problem-solving skills.
	Recognize priorities and take action tomake productive use of time.


Salary
N250,000 &ndash; N300,000 Monthly Net.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to:&nbsp;metusmetus73@gmail.com&nbsp;or&nbsp;transnationalzamiva21@yahoo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more information, WhatsApp no:&nbsp;09060194404.
	Preference will be given to candidates living in close proximity to MOWE, Ikeja,Isheri, Ojodu-Berger,Alausa,Ojota,Ketu,Magodo,Omole,Magboro and environs.
	Willingness to relocate to Mowe, Ogun Stateat the candidate&#39;s expense is also welcome
	Candidates without procurement experience should not apply.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-22",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Transnational Zamiva Services"
    
  },

Procurement Executive Job at Zamiva Transnational Services




        
    

        
        Zamiva Transnational Services is a full-fledged human capital development and management consultancy servicing company incorporated in August, 2021 with its corporate Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to ensuring quality value addition to our clients’ businesses spanning all sectors of the economy in the areas of HR consulting, outsourcing, talent acquisition, executive search, employee/reference background verification, payroll management, learning & development, renting, catering and janitorial services, etc., leveraging research, technology, a highly skilled and passionate human capital base, and our core values of Collaboration, Leadership, Integrity, Continuous Improvement, High Performance and Expertise.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Executive

Job Location: Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Assists the the Manager in leadingand implementing the procurement of materials, components, equipment, and services at favorable pricing.
	Ensures suppliers are consistent with scheduled deliveries and maintain specifications for applicable materials/equipment.
	Supports in supplier selection and pre-qualification process. 
	Investigates and recommends sources of supply and purchases through contractual arrangements to affect cost savings.
	Obtains sources, quotes, generates, and processes requisitions, and maintains related records, logs, files, artwork as pertained to the purchasing department.
	Check all purchasing invoices for accuracy in billing.
	Presents vendor cost comparison reports to upper management as requested. • Monitor and forecast upcoming levels of demand and track inventory levels.
	Other assigned tasks as necessary assigned by Management.
	Follows all safety regulations and complying with SOP's, EPA, OSHA, FDA, and cGMP's while performing duties. Maintain cleanliness and appearance of work area.
	Well versed in analysis of spend data and cost models for continuous improvement.
	Maintain relationships with various functional groups across the organization including Manufacturing, Quality, Distribution, and Administration.
	Other duties as assigned. Required


Required Education and Experience


	BSC / HND in any relevant field. However, a practical pharmaceutical manufacturing procurement experience is an advantage
	2 - 3 years of Procurement experience
	Extensive knowledge of cGMP, FDA, and other pharmaceutical requirementsis an advantage
	Experience of supply chain systems in developing contexts and logistics.
	Successful experience in audit/assessments and the implementation of supply chain management improvementsis an advantage.
	Ability to identify trends withinindustry.
	Ability to establish supplier specifications, measure conformance, and identify process improvement opportunities.
	Knowledge of risk management, with the ability to identify and address anticipated issues. • Ability to work independently and troubleshoot challenges.
	Must possess strong verbal, written, and oral communication skills.
	Must be able to work independently and with a team.
	Must possess problem-solving skills.
	Recognize priorities and take action tomake productive use of time.


Salary
N250,000 – N300,000 Monthly Net.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more information, WhatsApp no: 09060194404.
	Preference will be given to candidates living in close proximity to MOWE, Ikeja,Isheri, Ojodu-Berger,Alausa,Ojota,Ketu,Magodo,Omole,Magboro and environs.
	Willingness to relocate to Mowe, Ogun Stateat the candidate's expense is also welcome
	Candidates without procurement experience should not apply.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Human Resource Officer",
  "description": "Erisco Foods Limited is an indigenous food product manufacturer, incorporated in Nigeria in 2004 and started operations in 2009, manufacturing Tomato Paste, Seasoning, Rice, Milk cubes and more. We are firmly committed to producing high-quality products that combine nourishment with value pricing, as they strive to become one of Nigeria&rsquo;s largest indigenous foodmanufacturers on a global scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Human Resource Officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will support the HR department in implementing policies, managing recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and ensuring compliance with company standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Support employee onboarding, training, and development programs.
	Handle employee relations and promptly address staff concerns.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Assist with performance management processes.
	Provide HR reports to management as required.
	Manage full-cycle recruitment: sourcing, screening, interviewing, and onboarding.
	Develop and update job descriptions and job ads across platforms.


Requirements


	B.Sc. / HND qualification in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	2 - 3 years of relevant HR experience (FMCG/Manufacturing industry experience is an advantage).
	Strong knowledge of labor laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite.
	Ability to work under pressure and maintain confidentiality.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@eriscofoodsltd.com.ng&nbsp;using &quot;Human Resource Officer&quot; as subject of the mail.

Human Resource Officer Job at Erisco Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Erisco Foods Limited is an indigenous food product manufacturer, incorporated in Nigeria in 2004 and started operations in 2009, manufacturing Tomato Paste, Seasoning, Rice, Milk cubes and more. We are firmly committed to producing high-quality products that combine nourishment with value pricing, as they strive to become one of Nigeria’s largest indigenous foodmanufacturers on a global scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Human Resource Officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will support the HR department in implementing policies, managing recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and ensuring compliance with company standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Support employee onboarding, training, and development programs.
	Handle employee relations and promptly address staff concerns.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Assist with performance management processes.
	Provide HR reports to management as required.
	Manage full-cycle recruitment: sourcing, screening, interviewing, and onboarding.
	Develop and update job descriptions and job ads across platforms.


Requirements


	B.Sc. / HND qualification in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	2 - 3 years of relevant HR experience (FMCG/Manufacturing industry experience is an advantage).
	Strong knowledge of labor laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
	Proficiency in MS Office Suite.
	Ability to work under pressure and maintain confidentiality.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Human Resource Officer" as subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidate will be contacted.
